I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_OMI_AMOC_26n_profile, GLOBAL_OMI_AMOC_max26n_timeseries 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) is calculated at 26.5 oN by integrating 
the meridional velocity v across the Atlantic along the nearest latitude line to 26.5 oN and then 
doing a cumulative integral in depth. Hence the AMOC profile P each month is given by: 
𝑃(𝑧, 𝑡) =  ∫ ∫ 𝑣(𝑥, 𝑧′, 𝑡) 𝑑𝑥
𝑤𝑒𝑠𝑡
𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑡
𝑑𝑧′
𝑧
0
 
From this the monthly AMOC time series M is calculated as the maximum of P in depth each 
month. A climatological profile is calculated by taking the time mean of P from 1993 to the 
end of 2023. 
 
I.3 CMEMS products used as input for the computations 
 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_00
1_031 
QUID : Foreword (copernicus.eu) 
PUM: CMEMS-GLO-PUM-001-031.pdf (copernicus.eu) 
A comparison is made to an observational product (RAPID) of the AMOC which started in April 
2004. Details of the observa tional product can be found in Moat B.I. et al, 202 2 and the data 
downloaded from Smeed et al (2017) https://rapid.ac.uk/rapidmoc/rapid_data/datadl.php.   
 
 CMEMS Quality Information Document for 
AMOC 
Issue : 3.0 
2024/11 
 
 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
5 
II SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION 
A multi-product approach is used to construct OMIs for the AMOC (see Jackson et al, 2018). 
Four ocean reanalyses (GloSea5, ORAS5, C -Glorsv7, Glorys2v4) are used to calculate time 
series of the AMOC ( M) and climatological mean profiles ( P). These four reana lyses are then 
combined to form the Global Reanalysis Ensemble Product (GREP) with an ensemble mean 
and spread (2 x ensemble standard deviation) and compared to an observational product 
(RAPID).  
Results in Fig 1 show a profile that is mostly consistent wi th the observational product over 
the same time period. All individual products and the GREP show too little volume transport 
near the surface resulting in a profile that is weaker that than the observations in the upper 
800m. The maximum of the profile ag rees well with the observations and the deep flow is 
consistent with the observations, although this is a result of the agreement of one reanalysis 
(CGLORS) while the others have a too shallow return flow. It should be noted, however, that 
significant differences in the deep profile from RAPID observations are expected because of 
the observational methodology (Roberts et al, 2013; Stepanov et al 2016). 
The timeseries of M (Fig 2 top panel) also show agreement with the observations, with a 
correlation of 0.82, root mean square error of 2.0 Sv (1 Sv = 10 6 m3/s) and bias of 0.43 Sv for 
the monthly values of the multi -product over the observational period (Apr 2004 -Mar2016). 
Applying a

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the Ocean Monitoring Indicator for the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) at 26.5 degrees North, comparing model outputs with observational data from RAPID.
